CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-09-2016 Set For Trial On Sep-26-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Mar-23-2016 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-18-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Matter heard at 2:00 p.m. No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TRAVELERS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY OF AMERICA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. Plaintiff Editions Limited West, Inc. has pled facts sufficient to state claims for breach of contract, bad faith and declaratory relief. The demurrer presumes that as a matter of law Li's claims must be construed only to arise out of "advertising injury," then argues that the Publishers and Printers Liability endorsement, which excludes coverage for advertising injury claims arising out of "the publication or printing of any written or printed material." The Li complaint includes an allegation that Plaintiff made images of Piano Coloratura available for viewing in its on-line galleries and stores, which constitutes a public display of the infringing work. (FAC, 18.) Plaintiff has adequately pled that such allegations and the related claims could be characterized as "web site injury." Because the Publishers and Printers Liability endorsement does not cover "web site injury," it cannot be said that as a matter of law the Publishers and Printers Liability endorsement bars any possibility for coverage under the Travelers policies. The court did not consider Defendant's argument that the "Website Intellectual Property" exclusion bars any possibility of coverage on Li's claims because (1) it was first raised on reply and (2) Defendant did not identify the text of the exclusion or even direct the court to its whereabouts in the record. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332 (302/JMQ)
